name: Full Project Lifecycle with Poetry
description: Manage Python project dependencies, virtual environments, versioning, and publishing with Poetry's declarative pyproject.toml workflow.
prerequisites: pipx or uv installed (recommended for Poetry install)

steps.title: Install Poetry
steps.description: Install Poetry in an isolated environment using pipx. This prevents Poetry's own dependencies from conflicting with your projects.
steps.warning: Do not install Poetry with pip into a project virtualenv. It should be installed globally via pipx or the official installer.

steps.title: Create a new project
steps.description: Scaffolds a new project with pyproject.toml, a package directory, and a tests directory. The generated structure follows Python packaging best practices.

steps.title: Add production dependencies
steps.description: Adds packages to the [tool.poetry.dependencies] section and installs them. The poetry.lock file is updated automatically.

steps.title: Add development dependencies
steps.command: poetry add --group dev pytest ruff mypy coverage
steps.description: Adds packages to the dev dependency group. These are excluded when building distribution packages.
steps.tip: You can create custom groups like --group docs for documentation tools.

steps.title: Run commands inside the environment
steps.description: Execute any command within the project's virtual environment without manually activating it.

steps.title: Build and publish the package
steps.command: poetry version patch && poetry build && poetry publish --dry-run
steps.description: Bump the version, build source and wheel distributions, and preview a publish to PyPI. Remove --dry-run when ready to publish for real.
steps.warning: You need a PyPI account and API token configured via 'poetry config pypi-token.pypi <token>' before you can publish.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Full Poetry project workflow
set -e

PROJECT_NAME=${1:-myproject}

# Install Poetry if not present
if ! command -v poetry &> /dev/null; then
  pipx install poetry
fi

poetry new "$PROJECT_NAME"
cd "$PROJECT_NAME"

poetry add requests flask sqlalchemy
poetry add --group dev pytest ruff mypy coverage

poetry run pytest tests/
poetry build

echo "Project '$PROJECT_NAME' built with Poetry."
ls -la dist/

faq.question: What is the difference between poetry.lock and pyproject.toml?
faq.answer: pyproject.toml declares your dependency constraints (e.g., requests>=2.28). poetry.lock pins every dependency to exact versions, including transitive dependencies. Always commit both files.

faq.question: How do I switch Python versions in a Poetry project?
faq.answer: Use 'poetry env use python3.12' to create a new environment with a specific Python version. The python version must already be installed on your system (use pyenv to manage installations).

faq.question: Should I use Poetry or uv for new projects?
faq.answer: For speed and simplicity, uv is increasingly popular. For mature plugin support and PyPI publishing workflows, Poetry is well-established. Both produce standard pyproject.toml files.
